learning_outcomes.description: Explain the role and the limitations of occupational health and safety (OHS) management systems and OHS theories to a range of stakeholders.
Critically analyse and apply knowledge of systems and systems thinking to develop elements of an OHS management system in accordance with relevant standards and organisational requirements.
Develop performance indicators and explain to key stakeholders the rationale underpinning their development.

Develop processes to monitor and evaluate the impact of business, technology and industrial factors or conditions on OHS and OHS-related decision-making, ensuring decisions are made based on accurate data.
Make recommendations for criteria to measure OHS performance and develop processes to monitor and evaluate the effectiveness of an OHS management system, including audits.

Critically analyse and apply concepts of safety culture and the role of leaders in creating culture to develop strategies to influence the organisational culture and resultant decision-making affecting OHS.
Motivate and lead others within a team of OHS personnel, encouraging and empowering others.
Develop team building and teamwork skills during Group Work activities

assessments.task_description:
Students will be presented with an OHS management case study based on industry practice and will be required to apply the theory covered during the semester to the case study. The written component of the assignment has a word limit of 2500 words.

Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT)

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Online submission by Turnitin only by the due date and time. Refer to Blackboard for the submission link. No hard copy or assignment cover sheets are required. Submission via email is not accepted.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
Students will be required to complete an audit, as a group, for a UQ School/Institute/Division following established audit criteria. The report will be provided to the UQ HSW Manager, as well as being submitted as an assignment. The assignment has a word limit of 4000 words.

Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT)

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Online submission by Turnitin only by the due date and time. Refer to Blackboard for the submission link. No hard copy or assignment cover sheets are required. Submission via email is not accepted.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
End of Semester Theory Exam - content assessed from the whole semester: short answer and short essay-style questions. 

Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT)

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

learning_activities.topic:
Introduction to OHS Management Systems

What is an OHS Management System (OHSMS)? History of OHSMSs. Characteristics of an OHSMS.
learning_activities.topic_description: Introduction to OHS Management Systems What is an OHS Management System (OHSMS)? History of OHSMSs. Characteristics of an OHSMS.

learning_activities.topic:
Management System Frameworks

Workshop to explore OHSMS frameworks in detail and how to apply them to the development of an OHSMS.
learning_activities.topic_description: Management System Frameworks Workshop to explore OHSMS frameworks in detail and how to apply them to the development of an OHSMS.

learning_activities.topic:
Developing OHS Management Systems

Workshop on developing OHS Policy and Procedures.
learning_activities.topic_description: Developing OHS Management Systems Workshop on developing OHS Policy and Procedures.

learning_activities.topic:
Audit Planning and the Audit Process

Workshop on the audit process, including planning for audits and the audit steps.
learning_activities.topic_description: Audit Planning and the Audit Process Workshop on the audit process, including planning for audits and the audit steps.

learning_activities.topic:
Audit Evidence and Audit Reporting

Second workshop on practical aspects of auditing.
learning_activities.topic_description: Audit Evidence and Audit Reporting Second workshop on practical aspects of auditing.

learning_activities.topic:
Leadership and Organisational Culture

Workshop on the role of safety leadership and organisational culture on OHS performance.
learning_activities.topic_description: Leadership and Organisational Culture Workshop on the role of safety leadership and organisational culture on OHS performance.

learning_activities.topic:
Learning Organisations - Performance Monitoring

Workshop will look at the use of performance indicators in managing OHS.
learning_activities.topic_description: Learning Organisations - Performance Monitoring Workshop will look at the use of performance indicators in managing OHS.

learning_activities.topic:
Systems Thinking

Workshop to further explore the concepts of system thinking and complex systems.
learning_activities.topic_description: Systems Thinking Workshop to further explore the concepts of system thinking and complex systems.

learning_activities.topic:
Management of OHS Case Sudies

Workshop including guest speakers from industry presenting best practice case studies.
learning_activities.topic_description: Management of OHS Case Sudies Workshop including guest speakers from industry presenting best practice case studies.

learning_activities.topic:
Safety-I and Safety-II

This workshop will explore the Safety-I and Safety-II theory developed by Erik Hollnagel and includes a guest speaker from Griffith University.
learning_activities.topic_description: Safety-I and Safety-II This workshop will explore the Safety-I and Safety-II theory developed by Erik Hollnagel and includes a guest speaker from Griffith University.

learning_activities.topic:
Behaviour Based Safety Programs

This workshop will discuss the pros and cons of a behaviour-based safety approach to OHS management.
learning_activities.topic_description: Behaviour Based Safety Programs This workshop will discuss the pros and cons of a behaviour-based safety approach to OHS management.

learning_activities.topic:
OHS Influence Tactics

This workshop will cover different influence tactics that OHS professionals can employ to effect needed change in the workplace.
learning_activities.topic_description: OHS Influence Tactics This workshop will cover different influence tactics that OHS professionals can employ to effect needed change in the workplace.

learning_activities.topic:
Review for Exam

Closing lecture and review for end of semester exam.
learning_activities.topic_description: Review for Exam Closing lecture and review for end of semester exam.
